title: Rash

presenting_history.text: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports broad pediatric rash terminology and symptom grouping without supporting an individual diagnosis.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

red_flags.text: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: Defines the exact pediatric Dubai telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

red_flags.text: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports persistent, recurrent, febrile, immunocompromised, uncertain, stiff-neck, photophobia, and confusion escalation contexts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

medication.text: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.
medication.rationale: Directly supports mapped duration, evolution, distribution, itching, pain, family, medication, exposure, travel, and visual-assessment documentation.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

source_limitations: The viral-exanthem diagnosis label and allergic or infectious aliases are not treated as source-supported diagnoses.
Several baseline red flags are not stated in the reviewed exact sections and remain unsupported.
Medication and skin-care options remain clinician-entered and unsupported by this mapping.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
